bibliographic_data.title: Improving protein succinylation sites prediction using embeddings from protein language model
bibliographic_data.abstract: Protein succinylation is an important post-translational modification (PTM) responsible for many vital metabolic activities in cells, including cellular respiration, regulation, and repair. Here, we present a novel approach that combines features from supervised word embedding with embedding from a protein language model called ProtT5-XL-UniRef50 (hereafter termed, ProtT5) in a deep learning framework to predict protein succinylation sites. To our knowledge, this is one of the first attempts to employ embedding from a pre-trained protein language model to predict protein succinylation sites. The proposed model, dubbed LMSuccSite, achieves state-of-the-art results compared to existing methods, with performance scores of 0.36, 0.79, 0.79 for MCC, sensitivity, and specificity, respectively.

core_content.novelty_claim: The proposed model combines features from supervised word embedding with embedding from a pre-trained protein language model to predict protein succinylation sites, achieving state-of-the-art results.
core_content.key_methodology: A deep learning framework using embeddings from ProtT5 and supervised word embedding is employed to predict protein succinylation sites.
outcomes: Achieves state-of-the-art performance with MCC, sensitivity, and specificity scores of 0.36, 0.79, 0.79 respectively
Proposed model, LMSuccSite, serves as a valuable resource for exploring succinylation and its role in cellular physiology and disease
core_content.full_text_markdown: Protein succinylation is an important post-translational modification (PTM) that plays a crucial role in various physiological processes. This paper presents a novel approach combining supervised word embedding with embeddings from the protein language model ProtT5-XL-UniRef50 to predict protein succinylation sites, achieving state-of-the-art results. The proposed method uses deep learning techniques and demonstrates high accuracy in identifying succinylation sites.
